content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two integers X and K, where X is the length of the segment and K is the step number.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the coordinate of the K-th point Chef will put on a single line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The first test case has a segment of length 10 and K = 1. The middle point of the segment is 5, so the output is 5.000000. The second test case has a segment of length 10 and K = 2. The middle point of the left half of the segment is 2.5, so the output is 2.500000. The third test case has a segment of length 10 and K = 3. The middle point of the right half of the segment is 7.5, so the output is 7.500000. The fourth test case has a segment of length 1000000000 and K = 1234567. The output is 177375316.619873.
The segment has a length of 1 and K = 1. The middle point of the segment is 0.5, so the output is 0.500000.
The segment has a length of 1000000000 and K = 1. The middle point of the segment is 500000000, so the output is 500000000.000000.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by simulating the process of Chef choosing the subsegment and putting the point in its middle. We can use a priority queue to store the subsegments and their lengths. In each step, we pop the subsegment with the maximum length from the queue, put the point in its middle, and push the two new subsegments back into the queue.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * log K) per test case, O(T * log K) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(log K) — the space used by the priority queue.
